title: collomia tinctoria f. subulata
intro: Collomia tinctoria f. subulata, a variant of the Collomia tinctoria plant, is a flowering perennial plant native to North America, known for its vibrant purple flowers and edible, medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Collomia tinctoria f. subulata are said to provide relief from colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as reducing fever and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, salves, and tinctures, allowing for versatile treatment options and making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine.
